cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about regarding the Beijing 2008 Olympic medals. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to refer back to the text. The relevant part says: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee(IOC)were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The Shanghai Mint raised that standard to 3- meters. Medals can survive a 3-meter drop test with only minor cracks on the jade rings." So the key points here are that the IOC was concerned about the fragility of the jade rings. They wanted them to be strong enough to handle a 2-meter drop, and the mint made it even higher. The concern is specifically about the jade breaking or being fragile. Looking at the options, option D says "jade is fragile and easy to break up." That directly relates to the concern mentioned in the text. Option A is about the medals being worth less.
